content.principles: 1. Observer: Subject maintains a list of observers. Direct coupling (Subject knows about Observers). Synchronous usually. 2. Pub-Sub: Uses a Message Broker/Event Bus. Producers and Consumers are decoupled. Asynchronous often. 3. Difference: In Pub-Sub, components don't know each other; in Observer, they typically do.
pitfalls: Memory leaks: Observers not being removed when they are no longer needed.
Circular dependencies in complex Observer chains.
Debugging difficulty in Pub-Sub due to loose coupling and async execution.
